title: Homs is pawn in complex Syria talks amid reports of starvation
strengths: Direct, tightly bounded trapped-population figure for the final siege pocket.
limitations: The 2,500 figure is a mixed trapped-population count, not a clean civilian-only denominator.
notes: Used as the clearest opening node for the final 2014 pocket.

title: Syria evacuates civilians from besieged Homs center
strengths: Gives a clean first evacuation count explicitly labeled as civilians.
limitations: Covers only the first convoy and not the full pocket.

title: Six hundred Syrians flee besieged Old Homs in aid convoy
strengths: Provides both the second-wave evacuation total and a restatement of the 2,500 stranded figure.
limitations: Makes clear that some evacuees were men of fighting age whom authorities suspected of rebel affiliation.
notes: Used to show why trapped and evacuated totals cannot be cleanly civilian-coded.

title: Syrian authorities question over 300 men from besieged Homs - U.N
strengths: Sharpens the composition problem by showing that a large share of evacuees were men of fighting age under security questioning.
limitations: Still not a casualty source and not a direct death source.
notes: Used to reject a naive civilian-only denominator.

title: Living under Siege
geographic_scope: Old Homs and other besieged Syrian areas
strengths: Authoritative rights-focused confirmation that Old Homs civilians suffered severe hardship and deaths during more than 600 days of siege.
limitations: The cited deaths are not separated into direct violent versus starvation/medical-collapse categories for baseline DR purposes.
notes: Used to justify exclusion of siege deprivation deaths from dr_v1_direct.

title: Rebels evacuated from Homs, cradle of Syrian uprising
strengths: Provides the best located account of the final evacuation composition, including that 1,900 people were being evacuated and were mainly rebel fighters.
limitations: This is an evacuation/composition source, not a direct death source.
notes: Used to show that the late pocket was no longer a civilian-only trapped population.

title: Assad's forces take Homs, 'capital of Syrian revolt'
strengths: Useful synthesis source that states both the final takeover timing and that government forces had laid siege to the Old City a year earlier.
limitations: The 1,200 departing figure still merges fighters and trapped civilians.
notes: Used for conflict-level start and end anchors and to confirm late-pocket mixed composition.
